definition: Portfolio construction models embed 1998-2021 negative stock-bond correlations by institutional mandate (ALM mandates, risk parity frameworks, mean-variance optimization), making this a structural mispricing that is slow to correct. 60/40 portfolio risk increases ~50% under persistent positive correlation. Demographics-as-regime-change is unpriced in credit default models, vol models, and multi-asset risk budgets.
evidence: Portfolio construction models embed 1998-2021 correlations by institutional mandate
60/40 portfolio risk increases ~50% under persistent positive correlation
UK LDI crisis (2022) previewed correlation regime shift consequences
Mispricing is structural and institutional, not behavioral, therefore slow to correct
open_questions: How long will it take for institutional mandates to update correlation assumptions?
Which asset classes face the greatest repricing risk from correlation regime shifts?
Can dynamic hedging strategies mitigate the structural mispricing?
